Details: ESP32-C5, ESP32-C61, ESP32-H4, and ESP32-P4 are new additions since the 2024 ESP32 Guide
ESP32-E22 (Wi-Fi 6E tri-band) and ESP32-H21 (ultra-low-power BLE) — announced at CES 2026
Largely superseded by ESP32-S3. Not recommended for new designs
Only ESP32 with Bluetooth Classic. Hall sensor is deprecated in Boards Manager 3
No built-in Wi-Fi or Bluetooth — pairs with ESP32-C6 or similar as wireless companion
First ESP32 with dual-band (2.4/5 GHz) Wi-Fi 6. Mass production started April 2025
Cost-reduced version of ESP32-C6. Wi-Fi 6 + BLE but NO Zigbee/Thread. Mass production June 2025
Dual-core RISC-V with BLE 5.4 + 802.15.4. Targets wireless audio and Matter devices
Matter can run on chips with Thread or Wi-Fi. Marked ✓ for chips with native 802.15.4 (Thread) support
Only available on ESP32 (original) and ESP32-S2. Removed from all newer models
Capacitive touch on ESP32, S2, S3 (GPIO-based) and P4 (LP section). Not on C or H series
Full Speed (12 Mbps) on S2/S3. High Speed (480 Mbps) on P4. Other chips have USB Serial/JTAG only
Motor Control PWM available on ESP32, S3, C5, C6, and P4. Not on S2, C2, C3, C61, H2, H4
Maximum programmable GPIOs. Actual available pins depend on the module/devkit used
Approximate as of early 2026. Vary by vendor and region
Espressif datasheets and product selector (products.espressif.com). Verified against ESP-IDF documentation
